title: Sample Quality Compared
concept: VAEs tend toward blurry outputs; GANs produce sharp but sometimes less diverse outputs; Diffusion models often achieve both sharpness and diversity.

exercises.prompt: When you blur a photo with a smudge tool, what happens to the sharp edges?

options.text: They turn into new colors only

exercises.explain: Blurring softens sharp edges, making details fuzzy — exactly what happens with some AI-generated images!

cards.title: Which AI Draws Best?
cards.body: Imagine three artists: one paints soft dreamy pictures, one paints sharp but repeats the same face, and one paints crisp AND creative pictures every time. That's VAEs, GANs, and Diffusion models in a nutshell!

cards.title: Three Styles of AI Art
cards.body: VAEs (Variational Autoencoders) often make blurry, soft images. GANs (Generative Adversarial Networks) make sharp, realistic images but sometimes repeat similar-looking results. Diffusion models usually give sharp AND varied images, which is why they're so popular today.

cards.title: Three Painters in a Contest
cards.body: VAE is like a painter using a foggy brush — safe but blurry. GAN is like a painter who nails one perfect face but keeps painting it over and over. Diffusion is like a patient painter who slowly refines a foggy sketch into a sharp, unique masterpiece every single time.

cards.title: Generating Cat Photos
cards.body: Ask a VAE for a cat: you get a soft, slightly fuzzy cat shape. Ask a GAN: you get a sharp, realistic cat, but many outputs look suspiciously similar. Ask a Diffusion model: you get a sharp cat, and each one looks different — orange tabby, black cat, fluffy cat, all crisp and unique.

cards.title: Myth: Blurry Means Broken
cards.body: Myth: A blurry VAE image means the AI is broken. Truth: VAEs are just designed in a way that naturally smooths outputs — it's a trade-off for being easier to train and control, not a malfunction.

cards.title: Sharp vs Soft, Same vs Different
cards.body: Each model type balances sharpness and variety differently.

left.text: Soft, blurry, safe — but lacks fine detail

right.text: Sharp AND varied — best of both worlds

exercises.prompt: Which model type is most known for producing blurry images?
exercises.explain: VAEs tend to smooth out details, which often results in blurrier images compared to GANs and Diffusion models.

exercises.prompt: GANs can produce very sharp images but sometimes struggle to create diverse, varied outputs.
exercises.explain: True! GANs are famous for sharp realism, but they can suffer from 'mode collapse,' where outputs start looking too similar.

exercises.sentence: Diffusion models are popular today because they often achieve both sharpness and ___.
exercises.explain: Diffusion models are prized because they combine sharp detail with a wide variety of unique outputs.

exercises.prompt: You see an AI art collection: every image is crisp and detailed, but nearly every face looks eerily identical. Which model likely made these?
exercises.explain: Sharp images with repeated similar features is a classic sign of a GAN experiencing limited diversity, sometimes called mode collapse.

exercises.prompt: A researcher wants an AI model that generates crisp, highly detailed images AND a huge variety of different results for the same prompt. Which model type best fits this need, based on typical strengths?

options.text: VAE, because it's the simplest model
GAN, because it always creates diverse images
Diffusion model, because it balances sharpness and diversity well
None of these can produce sharp images

exercises.explain: Diffusion models are known for delivering both sharp detail and varied results, which is why they're widely used in modern image generation.
